question:
Why do societies generally view the silent treatment as a passive-aggressive rather than healthy form of conflict avoidance?
A) It is only passive-aggressive when used by men.
B) Silence is legally classified as verbal abuse in most countries.
C) It withholds communication as a form of punishment, preventing resolution while still asserting emotional dominance.
D) It is too noisy to be effective.
